title: Calibrate agent impact context on live bundles
goal: Measure the opt-in RepoBrief agent impact/edit context against the existing read-only workbench on coherent live bundles from representative repositories and decide retention, refinement, default promotion or removal without weakening authority boundaries.

approval.note: Use coherent, commit- and bundle-identity-bound live snapshots. Do not promote the action by changing a default route in the same measurement slice.

acceptance.assertion: The evaluation uses coherent live bundles from at least Lenskit, Grabowski and Weltgewebe, with every case bound to repository commit, snapshot id, run id and canonical dump digest.

The same fixed questions and expected targets are compared between the existing read-only workbench and agent impact context, recording target recall, context path count, missing-evidence visibility, latency and case-level regressions.
Graph edges, symbol paths and heuristics remain separately reported; a hit is not treated as answer correctness, runtime dependency, test coverage or complete blast radius.
Default promotion is a separate explicit decision and is allowed only when the registered aggregate threshold is met, no critical case regresses and human review confirms practical utility.
Goldset, observations, diagnostics, environment and provenance are committed or otherwise durably referenced so the result is reproducible and reviewable.
The measurement performs no source, Git, patch, pull-request, test, snapshot or memory mutation through RepoBrief and creates no review or merge authority.

metadata.reason: The committed synthetic contract fixture validates mechanics but does not establish utility on real repositories or justify default promotion.
execution_preconditions: live access to the rLens or equivalent Lenskit bundle runtime
coherent current bundles for Lenskit, Grabowski and Weltgewebe
representative real changes or questions with expected target sets
metadata.separate_decision_required: Any default-route promotion, API publication or broader agent-tool exposure must be proposed and reviewed after this measurement, not bundled into it.
